title: mRNA Lipid Nanoparticle Fights Tau Aggregation in Alzheimer's Disease
description: A lipid nanoparticle delivers TRIM11 mRNA to neurons via a neurotransmitter pathway, dissolving tau aggregates and restoring cognition in Alzheimer's mouse models with effects lasting three months after treatment.

title: Phil Newman: When Longevity Data Grows Up
description: Longevity.Technology founder Phil Newman discusses how longevity biotech is moving from narrative to execution as data maturity improves across the sector.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Reach $45.8B by 2033
description: New report values the longevity biotech market at $12.5B in 2026, projecting 20.4% CAGR growth to $45.8B by 2033, driven by AI-powered aging clocks and personalized longevity medicine.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: Detailed analysis of the longevity investment landscape for family offices, highlighting epigenetic reprogramming, senolytics, and organ regeneration as key themes in a $30.7B market.

title: ARDD 2026 Conference Relocates to Boston After Copenhagen Cancellation
description: The leading aging research conference ARDD 2026 relocates to Boston after a surprise cancellation of the Copenhagen venue, sparking rapid community response.

title: New Research Suggests Daily Multivitamins May Slow Biological Aging
description: ABC News medical correspondent reports on new research suggesting daily multivitamin supplementation may measurably slow biological aging markers.

title: Gut Bacterium Roseburia Linked to Increased Muscle Strength
description: Scientists find a positive correlation between the gut bacterium Roseburia inulinivorans and muscle strength in both mice and humans, opening new avenues for age-related muscle loss.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Comprehensive ranking of top longevity biotechs including Altos Labs, Cambrian Bio, and Genflow Biosciences, covering their latest funding rounds and pipeline progress.

title: Rapamycin Enters Phase 3 Human Trials via Cambrian Bio's $30.8M ARPA-H Award
description: Cambrian Bio receives $30.8M from ARPA-H to conduct the first Phase 3 trial of a next-generation rapamycin analog for healthspan extension, alongside new fasting mimetics research.

title: How Zinc Protects Injured Arteries From Accelerated Aging
description: Researchers discover that nuclei of cells lining injured arteries become misshapen, leading to accelerated senescence, and that zinc delivery partially alleviates this dysfunction.

title: Vitamin C Inhibits ACSL4 to Alleviate Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Prof. Horvath highlights March 2026 as a strong month for supplement longevity research, citing a Nature Medicine COSMOS trial paper and new vitamin C findings on ferro-aging in primates.

title: Puget Sound Startups Invest in the Science of Staying Healthy
description: High-profile backing from Jeff Bezos and Sam Altman is fueling Pacific Northwest biotech ventures focused on longevity and healthspan preservation.

title: Gut Bacteria Might Affect Cognition via the Vagus Nerve
description: Study suggests microbiome remodeling drives age-related cognitive decline through the vagus nerve, with antibiotics shown to reverse the effect in mice.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising in 2026
description: Analysis reveals the top five funded longevity startups (Altos Labs, Oura, Retro Biosciences, BioSplice, Insilico Medicine) account for ~$6.9B in total capital raised.

title: Scientists Successfully Freeze and Rewarm Mouse Brain Slices
description: German researchers vitrified and rewarmed mouse brain slices with much neuronal activity intact, advancing cryopreservation science in a PNAS-published study.

title: ARPA-H Devotes $144M to Studies That Slow Aging
description: ARPA-H's PROSPR program awards $144M to seven teams to build infrastructure for Phase 3 trials of aging interventions, including rapamycin, GLP-1 agonists, and SGLT-2 inhibitors.

title: Healthspan Horizons Advances Longevity Research with AI
description: Discussion of healthspan as one of the most important frontiers in modern healthcare, with AI driving advances in understanding biological drivers of long-term health.

title: UT Health $38M VITAL-H Trial: Rapamycin, Semaglutide, Dapagliflozin
description: Three FDA-approved drugs enter a major study to prove their capacity to slow biological aging, combining rapamycin, a GLP-1 agonist, and an SGLT-2 inhibitor in one trial.

title: Valter Longo Talks Fasting-Mimicking Diets at Stanford
description: USC gerontology professor Valter Longo presents at Stanford on fasting-mimicking diets and longevity research to transform healthy aging approaches.

title: Eric Verdin on the Future of Aging Research: CD38 Inhibitors and Creatine
description: Buck Institute CEO Eric Verdin discusses CD38 inhibitors that increase lifespan in mice and creatine's clear benefits for mitochondrial health and endurance.

title: Anti-Aging Therapy Market Targets $420B Milestone
description: Avai Bio and Austrianova begin production of a Master Cell Bank for Klotho anti-aging therapy under GMP standards as the broader anti-aging market eyes $420B.

title: Polyamine Longevity Compound May Also Fuel Cancer via eIF5A2
description: Scientists reveal polyamines have a dual life -- boosting healthy cells via eIF5A1 while turbocharging cancer through eIF5A2, with implications for supplement safety.

title: Linnaeus Wins $22M ARPA-H Award for LNS8801 Healthspan Preservation
description: Linnaeus Therapeutics secures $22M from ARPA-H's PROSPR program to test whether oral drug LNS8801 can preserve intrinsic capacity and independence as we age.

title: Longevity.Technology Quietly Becomes an AI Company
description: Longevity.Technology pivots toward AI-powered tools and platforms, signaling a strategic shift in the longevity media and data landscape.

title: Senosensitizers Clear Up to 70% of Zombie Cells in Preclinical Study
description: Cedars-Sinai preclinical study published in Aging Cell finds a new drug class, senosensitizers, enables senolytics to eliminate senolytic-resistant senescent cells that infections can reactivate.
